analysis.overall_assessment: The image shows a single leaf with some visible damage, indicating that it may be affected by pollution or disease.

pollution_reduction_solutions: Implement air filtration systems to reduce SO2 emissions
Regularly monitor and adjust soil pH levels

immediate_actions: Remove affected leaf from processing line
processing_recommendations.sorting_strategy: Separate healthy leaves from damaged ones
processing_recommendations.processing_method: Continue with standard processing for undamaged leaves, discard or treat damaged leaves separately
quality_preservation: Ensure proper sanitation of equipment to prevent cross-contamination

waste_prevention.salvageable_portions: Healthy leaf parts can be used for tea processing, while damaged portions should be discarded
waste_prevention.composting_guidelines: Mix with brown materials and turn regularly to ensure proper decomposition
prevention_measures: Regularly inspect and maintain processing equipment

economic_impact.estimated_loss_percentage: 1% of economic loss due to discarded leaf

priority_actions: Regularly monitor and adjust soil pH levels
Maintain proper sanitation of processing equipment
recommendations.timeline: Immediate actions to be taken within 1 week, long-term measures within 3 months
